intro: Ecbolium quinqueangulare, also known as Indian spurge or five-angled ecbolium, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium quinqueangulare include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Ecbolium quinqueangulare is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
